ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry specific to Kek Lapis Sarawak; closest match is the generic 'layer cake food product' class. Sarawak-specific geometric patterned layer cake is not individually represented.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00FL (Cakes and pastries) used as the closest defensible category. No specific FoodEx2 entry exists for Kek Lapis Sarawak.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Kek Lapis Sarawak or equivalent traditional Malaysian layer cake.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Kek Lapis Sarawak is one slice of approximately 50 grams, which provides around 240 kcal and is commonly enjoyed as a festive snack or gift item.

nutrient_source.title: Energy and Nutrient Composition of Foods — HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for butter cake / pound cake (FDC ID 18101) as closest analogous baked product
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB or USDA entry exists for Kek Lapis Sarawak. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore festive cake data and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy pound cake / butter cake (FDC ID 18101) as the closest approved analogous product. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 18.5g per 100g based on high butter and condensed milk content consistent with USDA butter cake profile. Values are consistent with the input record sourced from HPB Singapore. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, cholesterol, and water set to -1 as detailed fatty acid and moisture data for this specific traditional preparation are not individually reported in HPB Singapore or USDA FoodData Central databases as of 2024.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values — analogous estimate based on butter cake / sponge cake with refined sugar and white flour
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kek Lapis Sarawak. GI of 75 is an analogous estimate based on butter cake and sponge cake entries (GI ~70–80) in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables, consistent with the high refined sugar, white flour, and condensed milk composition of this product. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (75 × 20.75) / 100 ≈ 15.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (280 mg/100g) and high saturated fat from butter
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit Kek Lapis Sarawak to occasional small portions (≤50g) due to its moderate sodium content of 280 mg per 100g and high saturated fat load, both of which can contribute to elevated blood pressure and arterial stiffness.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (GI 75), high refined sugar (25.5g/100g), and high energy density (480 kcal/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kek Lapis Sarawak is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its high GI of 75, high refined sugar content of 25.5g per 100g, and dense caloric load; consumption should be strictly limited or avoided to prevent postprandial blood glucose spikes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~18.5g/100g from butter and condensed milk) and high energy density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The high saturated fat content of approximately 18.5g per 100g, derived primarily from butter and condensed milk, makes Kek Lapis Sarawak a high-concern food for individuals with cardiovascular disease; consumption should be minimised to reduce LDL cholesterol elevation risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High sugar and saturated fat content with potential pro-inflammatory effects
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Kek Lapis Sarawak to small occasional portions, as its high sugar and saturated fat content may contribute to systemic inflammation and exacerbate joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kek Lapis Sarawak, also known as Sarawak Layer Cake, is a visually striking and labor-intensive traditional cake originating from the Malaysian state of Sarawak on the island of Borneo. It is distinguished from the Indonesian Kue Lapis Legit by its vibrant food-coloured layers and intricate geometric patterns, which are achieved by baking many thin individual sponge layers and then cutting and reassembling them to create decorative cross-section designs. The cake is rich, dense, buttery, and moist, with a sweet flavour profile derived from condensed milk, sugar, and butter, often enhanced with warm spices such as cinnamon and cardamom or flavoured with chocolate or fruit purees. Each layer is baked separately under a grill or in an oven, making the preparation extremely time-consuming and skill-dependent. Kek Lapis Sarawak is a highly prized festive delicacy, commonly gifted and consumed during Hari Raya Aidilfitri and Gawai Dayak celebrations. Nutritionally, it is energy-dense at approximately 480 kcal per 100g, with high fat (32.5g, predominantly saturated from butter), high sugar (25.5g), and moderate carbohydrate (41.5g). Its high glycemic index of approximately 75 makes it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes or those managing blood glucose. The high saturated fat content raises cardiovascular concern. It is best enjoyed as an occasional festive treat in small portions.
common_user_queries: How is Sarawak Layer Cake made?
How many calories in Kek Lapis Sarawak?

What is the glycemic index of Kek Lapis Sarawak?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Kek Lapis Sarawak exists in HPB Singapore or USDA FoodData Central databases. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore festive cake data and cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy butter cake / pound cake as the closest approved analogous product. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database butter cake and sponge cake entries. Confidence is medium due to the absence of a direct approved-source match for this specific traditional preparation.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, cholesterol_mg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, cholesterol, and moisture data for Kek Lapis Sarawak are not individually reported in HPB Singapore or USDA FoodData Central databases as of 2024; these fields were confirmed unavailable after consulting both HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database and USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 18101). Saturated fat of 18.5g per 100g is estimated based on the high butter and condensed milk composition consistent with USDA SR Legacy butter cake profile (FDC ID 18101). GI of 75 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Kek Lapis Sarawak was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. Tamil and Hindi local names are not applicable as this is a Malaysian Sarawakian dish, not a Singaporean dish; these fields are left null. All core macros (protein_g 6.5g, fat_g 32.5g, carbohydrate_g 41.5g) are non-zero and nutritionally consistent with a high-butter, egg-rich, flour-based layer cake.
